rationale: The citing paper’s core mechanism is “ease of processing” and it tests disfluency-style boundary conditions (difficult language, low image quality, disfluent presentation), while the cited paper is a foundational experimental paper on preference/processing fluency and attribution effects. Given this strong construct-level alignment and the exact DOI match (10.1509/jmkr.44.3.347), this is plausibly a substantive theory/mechanism source rather than an incidental mention.
